Name: Nibelung Vassals
Anecdote: 'A thousand knights of the Nibelung land followed him, bold and well-clad; never did a hero bring a prouder following.'

Name: Chaplain in the Rhine
Anecdote: 'Hagen hurled the chaplain overboard to test the water-sprites' prophecy. The priest swam back to shore — and Hagen knew that not one of them would return.'

Name: Dankwart, the Marshal
Effect: On enter: If a friendly being was destroyed this turn, draw two cards.
Anecdote: 'Blood-spattered from the stair, Dankwart burst into the feast: You sit too long at table, brother. The dead are asking for you.'

Name: Alberich the Dwarf
Effect: On enter: Draw 'The Tarnkappe' or 'The Hoard of the Nibelungs' from your deck.
Anecdote: 'The mighty dwarf guarded the hoard beneath the mountain until Siegfried wrestled the cloak from him; thereafter he served the hero.'

Name: The Tarnkappe
Effect: While on top: Your beings at this location cannot be targeted by enemy card effects.
Anecdote: 'The cloak of darkness gave him the strength of twelve men and hid him from every eye. Unseen, he won Brunhild's games for Gunther.'

Name: Gunther, King of Burgundy
Effect: While on top: Gunther has +3 Power if 'Siegfried, the Dragon-Slayer' is at this location.
Anecdote: 'The king raised his arms as though he strained — but it was the unseen Siegfried who threw the stone and leapt beyond it.'

Name: Rüdiger of Bechelaren
Effect: On enter: Each opponent draws a card. You draw two cards.
Anecdote: 'The kindly margrave gave gifts to every guest — and at the last, gave his own shield to the man he was sworn to kill.'

Name: Volker, the Fiddler
Effect: While on top: Each opponent can play at most one being at this location each turn.
Anecdote: 'By day his fiddle-bow made sweet music; by night at the door it was edged with steel, and the Huns feared his tune.'

Name: Hagen of Tronje
Effect: On enter: You may destroy a friendly being. If you do, draw two cards.
Anecdote: 'On the hero's garment, between the shoulder-blades, was sewn a little cross of silk. Kriemhild had marked the place herself — for his protection, she believed.'

Name: Kriemhild, the Avenger
Effect: Kriemhild's power is equal to 2 + the number of humans in your underworld.
Anecdote: 'She wept for Siegfried thirteen years; then she married the king of the Huns, and her mourning became a plan.'

Name: Brunhild, Queen of Iceland
Effect: Cannot be played unless an enemy being with Power [4] or more is at this location.
Anecdote: 'Whoever would woo her must win three games — or lose his head. Twelve men groaned under the weight of her spear.'

Name: Burgundian Knights
Anecdote: 'A thousand and sixty knights rode with the kings to Etzel's land; not one of them came home again.'

Name: Siegfried, the Dragon-Slayer
Effect: Siegfried is immortal unless 'Hagen of Tronje' is at this location.
Anecdote: 'He bathed in the dragon's blood and no blade could bite him — save in one place, where a linden leaf had fallen between his shoulders.'

Name: Etzel, King of the Huns
Anecdote: 'The great king watched his hall burn around his guests, and could not say whether his grief or his wrath was greater.'

Name: The Hoard of the Nibelungs
Effect: While on top: At the start of your turn, get an extra mana crystal.
Anecdote: 'Twelve wagons in four days and nights could scarcely carry it. Hagen sank it all in the Rhine, and the two who knew the place kept silence forever.'
